name: France
summary: Day 85 (Saturday May 23). **EU SANCTIONS IRAN OVER HORMUZ BLOCKADE — AL JAZEERA MAY 22 SINGLE-SOURCE MENTION, REPORTED LOW-CONFIDENCE PENDING EUROPEAN-SIDE PRIMARY CONFIRMATION (Reuters Europe, Politico Europe, Euractiv, Le Monde, Der Spiegel have NOT corroborated through May 23 IST). If REAL, marks BREAKDOWN OF EUROPEAN 'WE'RE NOT IN THIS FIGHT' POSITIONING. France remains pre-positioned via CSG; no formal French foreign ministry statement on the alleged EU sanctions track.** France Charles de Gaulle CSG remains pre-positioned in Red Sea / Gulf of Aden following CMA CGM San Antonio strike; UK-France conditional Hormuz mission framework intact (51-nation Paris summit April 17); no operational change; CSG has NOT entered Hormuz proper. **The cleanest European media read**: no European institutional escalation in the public record, TTF easing slightly (€48.69 May 22), no major energy-security headlines today — European posture remains 'watching the US-Iran-Pakistan-Gulf track resolve, not intervening.' PRIOR (May 9): **FRANCE CHARLES DE GAULLE CSG REMAINS PRE-POSITIONED IN RED SEA / GULF OF ADEN — UK-FRANCE CONDITIONAL HORMUZ MISSION FRAMEWORK NOW MULTI-CONFIRMED AT 51-NATION PARIS SUMMIT (APRIL 17). NO OPERATIONAL CHANGE; CSG HAS NOT ENTERED HORMUZ PROPER.** Per USNI News, NBC, Defense News, Naval News and Euronews, FS Charles de Gaulle (R91) and escorts (FS Chevalier Paul, 1-2 FREMM-class frigates, fleet oiler FS Jacques Chevallier, possibly an SSN) remain pre-positioned. Mission described as 'would be entirely defensive and only deployed once lasting peace in the region was agreed' (CONFIRMED — USNI primary, NBC, Defense News, Naval News, Euronews, Aljazeera, US News, Washington Times, Business Standard). Trigger 'French CSG enters Hormuz proper' UNTRIPPED. PRIOR FRAMING (preserved as cycle-context): Macron leveraged technicality that CMA CGM San Antonio was Maltese-flagged not French-flagged to say 'France is in no way under attack' while simultaneously announcing CSG redeployment from East Med — Iranian 'negotiate while bombing' pattern mirrored. Russia/Pravda continues 'meddling' framing. Iran has not yet specifically responded to the conditional mission framework. The structural significance: this is the first formal European-autonomy posture of the cycle and it remains intact today with no escalation or retreat.

active_triggers.condition: France-Iran (Macron-Pezeshkian) channel emerges as parallel diplomatic track within 14 days (by 2026-05-21)
Iran or IRGC attacks European-flagged vessel in Hormuz within 14 days (by 2026-05-21) — direct France-Iran kinetic confrontation activation
French CSG enters Hormuz proper (transit through Strait, not just Red Sea/Gulf of Aden)

recent_signals.headline: FRANCE CHARLES DE GAULLE CSG TRANSITED SUEZ TO RED SEA / GULF OF ADEN FOLLOWING CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO STRIKE — FIRST EUROPEAN NAVAL SHIFT OF CYCLE; MACRON CALLED FOR HORMUZ REOPENING AND SPOKE WITH IRANIAN PRESIDENT PEZESHKIAN. CMA CGM San Antonio (Maltese-flagged, Philippine crew, French-owned shipping group) struck by cruise missile in Hormuz May 5; multiple crew injured. CBS attribution to Iran (single-source US officials, IRGC has NOT claimed). Macron: 'France is in no way under attack' (leveraged Maltese-flag technicality) while deploying CSG. The simultaneity (deploying force while denying attack) mirrors Iranian 'negotiate while bombing' pattern. The National UAE frames as Hormuz-reopening tailwind supportive of UAE position. ABC: French CSG specifically moved south of Suez 'ahead of conditional UK-French Hormuz mission.'
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED — French CSG redeployment multi-source (French armed forces primary + Naval News + ABC + NBC + Washington Times + US News); CMA CGM San Antonio strike = CONFIRMED ACTION (CMA CGM company statement primary); CLAIMED Iran attribution (CBS single-source US officials, IRGC has NOT claimed, no independent corroboration); Macron-Pezeshkian call = CONFIRMED (The Hill primary)
